document.diagnosis: Iteration 12 again shows that candidate generation is the primary bottleneck, while repair quality and reviewer variance determine how much the systems recover. Five-answer banks contained a correct answer on only 4/12 cases and nine-answer banks on 6/12; nevertheless, cross-examination and five-bank three-review repair each reached 5/12 through four helpful and zero harmful interventions. Weakest-family accuracy remained only 0.25 for those leaders, while nine-bank repair fell to 0.00 on logic despite matching 5/12 overall. The next batch should therefore replicate the positive mechanisms, preserve the nine-bank coverage probe, and directly test reviewer dilution without domain-specific changes.
most_important_evidence: Five-bank base plurality scored 1/12 and its candidate oracle only 4/12, but cross-examination and three-review repair each scored 5/12 with four helpful and zero harmful interventions; answer-generating review can exceed the raw-bank ceiling.

The exact one-review mechanism scored 4/12 in Iteration 12 with three helpful and zero harmful interventions. Pooled through four panels it is 18/54 exact with a 13-to-2 helpful-to-harmful intervention balance, making it the strongest call-efficiency probe.

The nine-answer bank improved candidate-oracle coverage from 4/12 to 6/12, but nine-bank repair still scored 5/12 and had 0/4 logic accuracy. More candidates improve coverage without reliably improving the weakest family.
Cross-examination and five-bank repair both had 0.25 weakest-family accuracy; none of the reviewed leaders solved the weakest-family problem robustly.

With a largest bank of nine and four three-review-equivalent systems at most, any six-strategy batch formed from the retained strategies and these proposals remains below 300 calls; the intended retained-plus-top-proposals configuration costs at most 228 calls.

proposals.hypothesis: An unchanged replication tests whether the established five-bank, three-review mechanism's 4-to-0 correction balance in Iteration 12 persists. Pairing it with the exact one-review mechanism on a shared bank isolates reviewer dilution.
proposals.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: The unchanged mechanism now has a pooled 13-to-2 helpful-to-harmful intervention balance while using two fewer review calls than three-review repair. Another sealed replication tests whether that advantage reflects lower reviewer variance rather than panel noise.
proposals.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Retaining the unchanged nine-bank mechanism tests whether its higher candidate-oracle coverage can translate into better weakest-family accuracy, rather than merely adding distractors for reviewers.
proposals.name: Nine Solvers plus Three Falsifying Repairers

proposals.hypothesis: Delegate fusion gives the deterministic base plurality one vote alongside three repaired outputs. It may preserve answer-generating corrections while reducing reversals or fragmentation when reviewers propose incompatible repairs.
proposals.name: Five-Bank Repair with Delegate Fusion

proposals.hypothesis: Three disjoint committee delegates compress a diverse nine-answer bank before one answer-generating reviewer is invoked on committee disagreement. This tests whether candidate overload, rather than insufficient bank coverage, causes review variance.
proposals.name: Committee-Delegated One-Review Repair

proposals.hypothesis: A deterministic nine-answer plurality isolates the value of bank expansion from all reviewer effects and provides a low-variance control for interpreting nine-bank repair.
proposals.name: Nine-Vote Plurality

research_hypotheses: The positive correction balance of falsifying repair is reproducible, but additional independent reviewers can dilute it by fragmenting among different repairs.
Increasing bank size raises candidate-oracle coverage, yet may not improve exact accuracy unless aggregation can manage the additional distractors.
Delegate fusion can protect a defensible base plurality without eliminating the ability of repairers to create an answer absent from the bank.
Committee delegation may reduce reviewer overload while preserving independent evidence from a larger bank.
Weakest-family gains must arise from domain-neutral improvements in candidate coverage and error correction, not from family routing.
document.risk_note: Only four cases per family make weakest-family accuracy move in 25-point increments, and the strategies sharing answer banks are strongly correlated. Positive intervention counts remain small, the nine-bank logic failure could be noise, and none of these development results establishes transfer beyond the three studied families.
